What we help with › Sports injuries
For all • All ages • Every stage of life

Sports injuries

Sporting or not

Whether it happened on the pitch, in the gym, at work or on the stairs, an injury is an injury, and we treat them all. From weekend twinges to problems that have stopped you training for months, we help people of every age and activity level recover properly and come back with confidence.

Understanding it

Every injury, sporting or not

The name says sports, but the door is wider than that. A twisted ankle on a kerb heals the same way as one twisted on a five-a-side pitch, and it deserves the same care. What matters is not how the injury happened but that it is assessed properly, treated well, and rehabilitated fully, because injuries that are rushed or ignored are the ones that come back.

We treat injuries to the knee, ankle, shoulder, elbow, wrist, hip, hamstring, calf, groin and everywhere between: sprains and strains, pulled and torn muscles, tendon and ligament problems, and the aftermath of accidents, falls, awkward lifts, collisions and surgery, including scar tissue, which can bind and restrict the layers beneath it and even interfere with how the area feels and moves long after the wound has healed. New or old, sudden or nagging, from rugby, running, racket sports, the garden or the workplace, we assess carefully, explain what we find, and build recovery around you and the demands you want your body to meet.

Get in touch

The easiest first step is a conversation. Tell us what is going on and we will point you the right way, with no pressure at all.

Call 07773 814488
What to expect

From first call to feeling better

What is likely going on

Most injuries follow recognisable patterns of overload or trauma. Working out exactly which tissue is involved, and how badly, shapes everything that follows.

How we assess it

A conversation about how it happened, then a careful look at the injury and how the rest of you moves around it, because injuries rarely exist in isolation.

What getting better looks like

A staged plan back to the things you do, whether that is a return to sport or simply a return to normal. Progressed and adjusted as you heal.

What it means for you

Ready for the next one

Laces tied, no second thoughts. Whether your comeback is a race, a return to the five-a-side league, or just trusting the stairs again, recovery is not finished until you are back doing the things you love without thinking about it.

The right care for this

Where this usually leads

Advanced Sports Therapy

The usual route for injury: expert assessment, hands-on treatment and structured recovery, whatever the injury and whoever you are.

Explore Advanced Sports Therapy ›

Integrated Rehabilitation

For injuries that need a longer road back, a structured, progressive programme that rebuilds strength, confidence and capability.

Explore Integrated Rehabilitation ›
Reassurance

Safe, gentle, and at your pace

Everything is done with care and at a pace that suits you, and nothing is rushed or forced. The aim throughout is simple: that you feel in good hands, understood, and steadily better.

What we help with

Recognised and accountable

Held to recognised professional standards

Members and registrants of the Royal Society of Medicine, the Complementary and Natural Healthcare Council, the Institute for Functional Medicine, the Society of Sports Therapists, the Institute of Sport and Remedial Massage and the British Medical Acupuncture Society, among others.
